title: Onboard HumbleStudio to the canonical repository development contract
summary: Adopt Jan's canonical repository development contract in HumbleStudio by adding repo-local AGENTS rules, a machine-readable repo contract, workflow docs, ticket and status surfaces, lane metadata, and shared validator wrappers without weakening the canonical policy.
acceptance_criteria: HumbleStudio has a repo-local AGENTS.md that adopts the canonical contract and documents repo-specific overrides explicitly.
Config/repo-contract.json declares the repo identity, workflow policy, module roots, localization strategy, and fixed worktree lane root.
docs/workflow-contract.md documents local git, ticket, verification, lane, and override rules truthfully.
Shared validator and repo doctor entrypoints exist locally, and HumbleStudio has repo-native ticket plus status surfaces.
Relevant validation commands pass or any branch-freshness gate failure is reported honestly as a promotion-context check.

evidence.note: User explicitly requested onboarding this repository to Jan's canonical development contract and asked for validation, staging, and a commit.
Validated the first HS ticket and local ticket schema.
The shared contract validator passed; it still reports the known custom localization-strategy warning.
Strict repo doctor is a branch-freshness gate and failed from the active topic branch because the reserved root checkout is intentionally not on clean main during this onboarding slice.
All local workflow scripts compiled successfully.
Diff whitespace checks passed for the onboarding slice.

trigger.source_ref: User asked to onboard HumbleStudio to the canonical repository development contract.

agent_notes: This repository did not have a local AGENTS.md or shared-contract scaffold before this onboarding slice.
Lane workflow was not available at the start of work; this slice introduces the lane metadata surface for future use.
